text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Everton, a peculiar old bookstore stood on Maple Street. Behind the cedar doors were shelves steeped in forgotten lore and dusty tomes that smelled of mold and mystery. That was home to Mr. Jasper Whittaker, a recluse with eyes as sharp as the oldest paws of the big cats that prowled outside.

Jasper's books never caught anyone's notice. They were the sort no schoolchild would ever dream of finding. Instead, they disappeared into the vastness of collectors’ boxes or became components of odd collections. But whispered legends among townsfolk spoke of Mr. Whittaker's extraordinary ability to know things without knowing how he knew them—like the name of the last person who passed through his shop or the smallest detail of an old sailor’s tale.

One misty morning, a young girl named Lily stumbled upon Jasper’s. There was something oddly comforting about his shop; the way the sun filtered through the dusty leaves creating
